q: What does the grammar pattern 'あるまじき' mean?
e: It's not a proper action for a professional artist to steal others compositions.

s: The grammar pattern 〜に難くない means 'not hard to ...(名詞＋にかたくない/　Vる＋にかたくない）'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜に難くない' mean?
a: not hard to ...(名詞＋にかたくない/　Vる＋にかたくない）
d: consequently and as a logical outcome
however and yet despite all this
to repeat the same action over
to attempt something again and again
after a long time has passed
e: not hard to imagine it becoming a problem

s: The grammar pattern なり means 'expresses continuing state'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'なり' mean?
d: not hard to ...(名詞＋にかたくない/　Vる＋にかたくない）
leads to, all the way to, as far as
Just as one is about to start doing something
e: he kept staring at her

s: The grammar pattern ならともかく means 'I'm not sure if it is [possible/so] but...'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'ならともかく' mean?
a: I'm not sure if it is [possible/so] but...
d: at the exact moment when something happens
then and only then will it occur
it is permitted and allowed to do so
you are required to complete this task
someone is supposed to act in this way
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
on the other hand from this perspective
e: It's possible that an expert could do this, but I can't.

s: The grammar pattern まして〜はずがない means 'it is even more unlikely that 〜'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'まして〜はずがない' mean?
a: it is even more unlikely that 〜
d: at the exact moment when something happens
then and only then will it occur
it is permitted and allowed to do so
you are required to complete this task
someone is supposed to act in this way
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
consequently and as a logical outcome
e: even adults also being eluded of this matter,there is no way/it is even more unlikely that children knows such a thing

s: The grammar pattern 〜てはいられない・〜てばかりはいられない means 'can't / can't afford 〜(doing something)'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜てはいられない・〜てばかりはいられない' mean?
d: cannot exist, not possible, can't afford to
Notwithstanding, even while, even though, as she was
Conveys the idea that if something occurs, then a bad end is reached.
e: we can't be relaxing like this drinking tea

s: The grammar pattern 不調法 or 無調法 means 'A rudeness or blunder, carelessness'
q: What does the grammar pattern '不調法 or 無調法' mean?
d: unless / impossible if it's not / only possible with/by/at/etc / very difficult by any other~,
e: (1)ainiku osake ha buchouhou deshite - I must apologize but I don't drink (2) karaoke wo utatte agerareru to ii n desuga, ainiku buchouhou na mono desu.　If I could sing karaoke to you I would but sorry unfortunately I can't.

s: The grammar pattern やむをえない (已むを得無い) means 'no other way to do something　しかたがない'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'やむをえない (已むを得無い)' mean?
a: no other way to do something　しかたがない
d: in line with, in keeping with
despite a certain time / despite a certain situation
Without really planning to, ~ A phrase indicating that the subject does something without paying much attention or that when, where, to whom, etc. s.t. took place is unclear.
not hard to ...(名詞＋にかたくない/　Vる＋にかたくない）
e: この大雪では、試合を中止にするのもやむをえない There's no other way but to cancel the game because of the snowfall

s: The grammar pattern そばから, 傍から means 'as soon as, right after'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'そばから, 傍から' mean?
d: In terms of both ~ and ~; in terms of ~ as well as ~; from the perspective of both ~ and ~
e: Pattern: Verb dictionary form+そばから, Verb past ~た form+そばから. The child messes up [the room] (repeatedly) as soon as I clean so I already became wanting to give up.

s: The grammar pattern てからというもの means 'Ever Since s.t. changed'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'てからというもの' mean?
e: Pattern: Verb て-form+からというもの. Ever since the daughter returned, her aging father has really perked up

s: The grammar pattern 矢先に means 'Just as one is about to start doing something'
q: What does the grammar pattern '矢先に' mean?
a: Just as one is about to start doing something
d: it's already too late, It's no use doing s.t. now
covered in st (名詞＋に＋まみれる); (名詞＋まみれ)
expressing the speaker's feeling that although an ideal action or state isn't possible, at least the next best action or stat is or should be available
moving from one battlefield to another
e: Just as I was about to leave the house, it started raining

s: The grammar pattern と言ったらない means 'There's no way to express...'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'と言ったらない' mean?
d: consequently and as a logical outcome
however and yet despite all this
to repeat the same action over
to attempt something again and again
after a long time has passed
e: There's no way to express my gratitude for your saving me

s: The grammar pattern 〜てまで means 'do as far as to'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜てまで' mean?
d: I'm not sure if it is [possible/so] but...
But...! / But If (you) had just... / But (I) could have...
e: Pattern: Verb て-form+まで. ～という程まで

s: The grammar pattern お茶を濁す means 'to avoid giving a direct answer'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'お茶を濁す' mean?
a: to avoid giving a direct answer
d: at the exact moment when something happens
then and only then will it occur
you are required to complete this task
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
consequently and as a logical outcome
however and yet despite all this
e: 本音を見せず、お茶を濁した。He beat around the bush and avoided showing his true feelings.//もちろん人それぞれだからと慌てて茶を濁し、話題をすり替えた。She hurriedly made up some excuse about there being all types of people and changed the subject.

s: The grammar pattern ふと means 'suddenly, by coincidence'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'ふと' mean?
e: without thinking, I shouted out

s: The grammar pattern せいぜい (精精) means 'my utmost, at most'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'せいぜい (精精)' mean?
d: "thought so, but" OR, "would have thought so/imagined, but didn't"
Once [things have] reached the point at which...; (Only) now that it has come to this...
e: try my utmost

s: The grammar pattern 〜だに means 'Even; just'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜だに' mean?
e: I never even imagined it

s: The grammar pattern 〜が早いか means 'as soon as'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜が早いか' mean?
e: Pattern: Verb dictionary form+がはやいか. As soon as the President arrived

s: The grammar pattern 〜をものともせずに means 'In the face of, in defiance of, in spite of'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜をものともせずに' mean?
a: In the face of, in defiance of, in spite of
d: leads to, all the way to, as far as
No matter what; Even if; No matter how
e: 周囲の反対をものともせずに、二人は結婚した。

s: The grammar pattern あればこそ means 'only because'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'あればこそ' mean?
e: ~だからこそ（他の理由ではない）

s: The grammar pattern どうも means 'apparently, naturally'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'どうも' mean?
e: of course she got angry

s: The grammar pattern かたわら means 'besides doing a main thing, one is also involved in another activities.'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'かたわら' mean?
a: besides doing a main thing, one is also involved in another activities.
d: because/since - explains the cause or gives a reason for
Without really planning to, ~ A phrase indicating that the subject does something without paying much attention or that when, where, to whom, etc. s.t. took place is unclear.
would be much better, would be acceptable
e: Pattern: Verb dictionary form+かたわら, Noun+の+かたわら. the king sat beside the queen

s: The grammar pattern 気が付いた means 'I realized'
q: What does the grammar pattern '気が付いた' mean?
e: natto is tasty, I realized

s: The grammar pattern がてら means 'while doing something and taking advantage of that opportunity to do something else'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'がてら' mean?
a: while doing something and taking advantage of that opportunity to do something else
d: Whether X or Y; A structure that means "no matter which one is the case"
Although it is said that, even though it is said, admitting that, that being said, however, nevertheless
e: Pattern: Noun+がてら, Verb ます stem+がてら. getting one thing done while doing something else

s: The grammar pattern 極まりない/　極まる means 'unlimited, extremely, boundlessly'
q: What does the grammar pattern '極まりない/　極まる' mean?
e: 私は、彼の失礼きわまりない態度に我慢ならなかった。 I couldn't get used to his unlimited rudeness.

s: The grammar pattern たかだか (高高) means 'At most'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'たかだか (高高)' mean?
e: At the very outside

d: If s.o. does s.t for even a moment, the following result will necessarily occur • Once someone starts something, the person can’t stop it
as if; as if ~ were convinced that; as if ~ believed that; as if ~ decided that
e: don't have the time to

s: The grammar pattern からすると means 'from (the point of view)'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'からすると' mean?
d: there is a tendency for ~ to happen (and it is somewhat bad and/or a cause for concern)
in sharp contrast with; compared to
Even only / Even merely / Even just
e: from his point of view

s: The grammar pattern せめて means 'at least, at most a small thing'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'せめて' mean?
a: at least, at most a small thing
d: despite a certain time / despite a certain situation
"It's not as if" giving a reason why something should not be how it is
e: at least I want to finish
